The **2013 NRL season** was the 106th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") club competition in Australia. The [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League")'s main competition, named the **2013 Telstra Premiership** after major sponsors [Telstra](/wiki/Telstra "Telstra"), was contested by sixteen teams during the regular season, which lasted from March to September, and resulted in the top eight finishing teams, who went on to contest the finals. The season culminated in the [Grand Final](/wiki/2013_NRL_Grand_Final "2013 NRL Grand Final"), in which the [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") defeated the [Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles](/wiki/Manly-Warringah_Sea_Eagles "Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles") 26-18 to win their first NRL premiership since [2002](/wiki/2002_NRL_season "2002 NRL season").

The NRL season started with the [2013 NRL All Stars match](/wiki/2013_NRL_All_Stars_match "2013 NRL All Stars match"), which was played in February. The [2013 Holden Cup](/wiki/2013_NRL_Under-20s_season "2013 NRL Under-20s season"), the NRL's Under-20s competition, also took place alongside the Premiership, with most matches held before the first grade competition. The Parramatta Eels picked up the wooden spoon for the second consecutive season after winning only five matches for the season.

## Season summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2013_NRL_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Season summary")]

See also: [2013 NRL season results](/wiki/2013_NRL_season_results "2013 NRL season results")

### Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2013_NRL_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga")]

See also: [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks_supplements_saga "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga")

The 2013 NRL season was marred by a major investigation involving the [Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks")' supplements program, which was alleged to have taken place during the [2011 season](/wiki/2011_NRL_season "2011 NRL season"). Following an almost year-long investigation by [ASADA](/wiki/Australian_Sports_Anti-Doping_Authority "Australian Sports Anti-Doping Authority") and the NRL, a series of penalties were applied on the club, including, among others, the 12-month suspension of [Shane Flanagan](/wiki/Shane_Flanagan "Shane Flanagan") as the club's head coach, as well as a [$](/wiki/Australian_dollar "Australian dollar")1,000,000 fine (with $400,000 suspended) and the deregistration of Trent Elkin as the club's trainer.[[1]](#cite_note-NRL-1)

Despite the off-field controversy, the Sharks were still able to reach the NRL finals for the second consecutive year, losing to eventual grand finalists Manly in the second week.

### Teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2013_NRL_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Teams")]

The lineup of teams remained unchanged for the 7th consecutive year. A report conducted by Brand Finance valued the Penrith Panthers club at $46.2m, the highest of any Australian sporting brand, while the Brisbane Broncos had the highest brand equity.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Finals series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2013_NRL_season&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Finals series")]

For the second year the NRL uses the finals system previously implemented by the ARL competition from the 1990s (also used as the [AFL final eight system](/wiki/AFL_final_eight_system "AFL final eight system")) to decide the grand finalists from the top eight finishing teams. Both the seventh-placed [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights") and minor premiers [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") return the finals after last featuring respectively in 2011 and 2010, whilst the other six teams (Bulldogs, Storm, Rabbitohs, Sea Eagles, Cowboys & Sharks) were featured in the preceding finals series.
